target.name: Vertical Semiconductor
target.founder: Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, Tomas Palacios

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vertical Semiconductor is a Seed-stage company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 8 private peers (including Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI at Series A) and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $28M based on the $11M raised proxy. The peer-median method anchors to four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies with valuations ranging from $500M to $5.0B; the median across this set is $1.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies at this stage. The public sector median P/S of 22.3x across the three tickers provides context but cannot be operationalized without revenue data.

Triangulating across the two applicable methods produces a range of $23M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $764M. The wide spread reflects the inherent uncertainty at Seed stage and the significant valuation variation within the peer set. Key drivers of valuation within this range remain the company's technical differentiation, capital efficiency trajectory, and path to meaningful revenue—none of which can be quantified from available data.
